behavior_summary: Defines behavior for a static rock item: when an actor 'looks' at the rock it emits a short description ('rock') to that actor.
When the rock is hit (gotHit), it spawns a FREE/debris process and is given motion via Item::hurl, turning the static rock into a moving/thrown object.
Both U8P_FALLROCK::look and gotHit wrap their actions with process/info-state changes and a process-exclude step (engine-level guards to prevent recursion or to mark processing state).
notable_control_flow: U8P_FALLROCK::FALLROCK::look: set process/info and process-exclude -> call Item::bark(target=viewer, "rock ") -> use return in an implies/pop sequence -> suspend/return.

U8P_FALLROCK::FALLROCK::gotHit: set process/info and process-exclude -> spawn FREE::1C19(subtype=0x3C, param=0) -> use spawn return in implies/pop -> call Item::hurl(args: 0x04,0,0,0, target=item) -> suspend/return.
open_questions: Exact numeric/info id used for the 'set info' operation and the precise semantics of that info key (only the effect of setting/excluding is observed).
Meaning of the spawn subtype byte 0x3C used with FREE::1C19 (inferred to be debris/particle but not proven).
Precise semantics of the Item::hurl argument list (0x04,0,0,0) — whether these encode speed, direction, or a preset motion type.
Whether any additional game-state/global flags are affected elsewhere by these routines (no global flags are read/written in this script excerpt).

main_function.name: U8P_FALLROCK::look
main_function.purpose: When a process/actor examines the rock, produce a short descriptive text ('rock') directed at that actor and adjust process/info state to guard the action.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_FALLROCK::look) — invoked when an actor views/inspects the rock (enterFastArea-style trigger of the item's U8P_FALLROCK::look event).

function_map.name: U8P_FALLROCK::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_FALLROCK::look' event: set per-process/info guard, call Item::bark to display 'rock ' to the viewer, then suspend.

function_map.name: U8P_FALLROCK::gotHit
function_map.role: Handle the 'gotHit' event: set guards, spawn a FREE-class debris/process, and invoke Item::hurl to impart motion to the rock.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine that emits a short text/message from an item to a target process/player.

function_map.name: spawn FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Engine spawn operation used to create a FREE-class process (likely debris/effect) associated with the rock being struck.

function_map.name: Item::hurl
function_map.role: Engine routine to apply motion/impulse to an item so it moves/flies after being hit.

function_map.name: U8P_FALLROCK::set info / process-exclude
function_map.role: Engine-level operations observed before actions: set per-object/process info and exclude the process from normal processing temporarily (guard behavior).

external_calls.purpose: Send/display the short description string 'rock ' to the viewing process (target passed via BP+06h).
Create a FREE-class process (inferred debris/effect) with subtype byte 0x3C and parameter 0 to represent rock breakage/debris.
Apply motion to the rock using arguments (0x04,0,0,0) so it is hurled/thrown after being struck.
Protect or mark the process/item state before/after barking to avoid recursion or indicate temporary processing exclusion.
Temporarily set info and exclude the process/item before performing spawn/hurl to protect state or prevent reentry.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Defines behavior for a static rock item: when an actor 'looks' at the rock it emits a short description ('rock') to that actor.
